title: QVHighlights: Detecting Moments and Highlights in Videos via Natural Language Queries
authors: Jie Lei, Tamara L. Berg, Mohit Bansal
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Primary STITCH Table 2 moment retrieval evaluation benchmark. The Moment-DETR baseline is also introduced here.
notes: Dataset paper with Moment-DETR baseline. 10K+ YouTube videos. R1@0.5/0.7, mAP metrics.

filename: Generic Event Boundary Detection_ A Benchmark for Event Segmentation.pdf
title: Generic Event Boundary Detection: A Benchmark for Event Segmentation
authors: Mike Zheng Shou, Stan Weixian Lei, Weiyao Wang, Deepti Ghadiyaram, Matt Feiszli
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Defines GEBD task and Kinetics-GEBD benchmark used in STITCH Table 1.
notes: Facebook AI + NUS. Rel.Dis. and F1 metrics. Foundational benchmark for STITCH's first task family.

filename: InternVideo2_ Scaling Foundation Models for Multimodal Video Understanding.pdf
title: InternVideo2: Scaling Foundation Models for Multimodal Video Understanding
authors: Yi Wang, Kunchang Li, Xinhao Li, Jiashuo Yu, et al. (Yu Qiao, Yali Wang, Limin Wang corresponding)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Frozen backbone for STITCH. Per-clip embeddings reused across all three task families.
notes: OpenGVLab Shanghai AI Lab. Multi-modal video foundation model.
evidence_basis: Pretrained_backbone=true and supervised=true because InternVideo2 itself is trained with multi-modal contrastive + masked objectives at scale.

filename: MLVU_ Benchmarking Multi-task Long Video Understanding.pdf
title: MLVU: Benchmarking Multi-task Long Video Understanding
authors: Junjie Zhou, Yan Shu, Bo Zhao, Boya Wu, Zhengyang Liang, Shitao Xiao, et al. (Zheng Liu corresponding)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: STITCH Table 3 long-video VLM reasoning benchmark.
notes: BAAI + BUPT. v1 had wrong title and wrong NeurIPS 2024 venue — corrected from PDF.

filename: Video-MME_ The First-Ever Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark of  Multi-modal LLMs in Video Analysis.pdf
title: Video-MME: The First-Ever Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark of Multi-modal LLMs in Video Analysis
authors: Chaoyou Fu, Yuhan Dai, Yongdong Luo, Lei Li, Shuhuai Ren, Renrui Zhang, et al.
closest_relation_to_our_paper: STITCH Table 3 VLM reasoning benchmark; long-video split is the relevant one.

filename: Practical and powerful kernel-based change-point detection.pdf
title: Practical and Powerful Kernel-Based Change-Point Detection
authors: Hoseung Song, Hao Chen
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Kernel-based CPD methodology paper. v1 conflated this with the ruptures library (Truong et al.) — they are distinct works. Whether STITCH's CPD specifically uses Song & Chen's kerSeg framework or the more common ruptures library needs verification against the actual STITCH draft.
notes: ⚠️ v1 misattributed this to Truong et al. The actual local PDF is Song & Chen, kerSeg (R package). Implements high-dimensional kernel CPD with permutation null. May or may not be STITCH's canonical citation — the actual citation depends on whether STITCH calls into ruptures or kerSeg.
evidence_basis: Demoted from core to supporting until it is verified that STITCH actually cites this specific paper rather than Truong et al. (ruptures) which v1 confused this with.

filename: Optimal detection of changepoints with a linear computational cost.pdf
title: Optimal Detection of Changepoints with a Linear Computational Cost (PELT)
authors: Rebecca Killick, Paul Fearnhead, Idris A. Eckley
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PELT algorithm provides O(n) exact change-point search; foundational algorithm for STITCH's segmentation efficiency.
evidence_basis: training_free=true and unsupervised=true because PELT is a parameter-free algorithmic procedure with no learned weights.

filename: Generic Event Boundary Detection via Denoising Diffusion.pdf
title: Generic Event Boundary Detection via Denoising Diffusion (DiffGEBD)
authors: Jaejun Hwang, Dayoung Gong, Manjin Kim, Minsu Cho
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised GEBD competitor in STITCH Table 1. Uses temporal self-similarity + diffusion to predict boundaries.
notes: POSTECH + GenGenAI. Methodologically interesting — also leverages temporal self-similarity, related conceptually to STITCH's kernel matrix.

filename: Rethinking the Architecture Design for Efficient Generic Event Boundary Detection.pdf
title: Rethinking the Architecture Design for Efficient Generic Event Boundary Detection
authors: Ziwei Zheng, Zechuan Zhang, Yulin Wang, Shiji Song, Gao Huang, Le Yang
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised GEBD competitor on Kinetics-GEBD; emphasizes inference efficiency.
notes: Xi'an Jiaotong + Tsinghua. Efficient architecture for GEBD.

filename: Online Generic Event Boundary Detection.pdf
title: Online Generic Event Boundary Detection (On-GEBD)
authors: Hyungrok Jung, Daneul Kim, Seunggyun Lim, Jeany Son, Jonghyun Choi
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Streaming/online GEBD competitor (no future frame access). Different from STITCH's offline batch CPD but useful contrast on online applicability.
notes: GIST + SNU + POSTECH. Defines new On-GEBD task variant.
evidence_basis: long_video_framing=true because targeting streaming/long-form video without buffering.

filename: Revisiting Kernel Temporal Segmentation as an Adaptive Tokenizer for Long-form Video Understanding.pdf
title: Revisiting Kernel Temporal Segmentation as an Adaptive Tokenizer for Long-form Video Understanding
authors: Mohamed Afham, Satya Narayan Shukla, Omid Poursaeed, Pengchuan Zhang, Ashish Shah, Sernam Lim
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Most architecturally similar prior work to STITCH — task-agnostic, unsupervised, KTS-based adaptive tokenizer on frozen features.
notes: Meta AI. Evaluated on long-form video understanding tasks (video classification, temporal action localization). STITCH's relationship: extends scope to GEBD, moment retrieval, and VLM frame selection.
evidence_basis: training_free=true, unsupervised=true, zero_shot=true confirmed in abstract: 'task-agnostic, unsupervised and scalable approach based on Kernel Temporal Segmentation'.

filename: Training-free Video Temporal Grounding using Large-scale Pre-trained Models.pdf
title: Training-free Video Temporal Grounding using Large-scale Pre-trained Models (TFVTG)
authors: Minghang Zheng, Xinhao Cai, Qingchao Chen, Yuxin Peng, Yang Liu
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Training-free temporal grounding baseline using frozen pre-trained models. Most directly comparable training-free moment retrieval method.
evidence_basis: training_free=true, zero_shot=true confirmed by paper title and abstract claim of leveraging frozen pre-trained models with no task-specific training.

filename: Zero-shot Video Moment Retrieval via Off-the-shelf Multimodal Large Language Models.pdf
title: Zero-shot Video Moment Retrieval via Off-the-shelf Multimodal Large Language Models (Moment-GPT)
authors: Yifang Xu, Yunzhuo Sun, Benxiang Zhai, Ming Li, Wenxin Liang, Yang Li, Sidan Du
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Tuning-free zero-shot moment retrieval with frozen MLLMs (LLaMA-3 + frozen MLLMs). Direct training-free baseline.
evidence_basis: training_free=true confirmed by 'tuning-free pipeline' phrasing in abstract.

filename: Zero-Shot Video Moment Retrieval from Frozen Vision-Language Models.pdf
title: Zero-Shot Video Moment Retrieval from Frozen Vision-Language Models
authors: Dezhao Luo, Jiabo Huang, Shaogang Gong, Hailin Jin, Yang Liu
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot frozen-VLM moment retrieval baseline. Direct training-free comparison target.
notes: Queen Mary U London + Adobe + Peking U.
evidence_basis: training_free=true, zero_shot=true confirmed in title and use of frozen VLMs.

filename: Wavelet-based Frame Selection by Detecting Semantic Boundary for Long Video Understanding.pdf
title: Wavelet-based Frame Selection by Detecting Semantic Boundary for Long Video Understanding (WFS-SB)
authors: Wang Chen, Yuhui Zeng, Yongdong Luo, Tianyu Xie, Luojun Lin, Jiayi Ji, Yan Zhang, Xiawu Zheng
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PROMOTED to core in v2. Training-free, semantic boundary-based frame selection for long video VLMs. Conceptually the closest published method to STITCH App 3 (VLM frame selection): both detect semantic narrative shifts query-agnostically without training.
notes: Xiamen U. Wavelet-based detection of semantic shifts for LVLM frame selection.
evidence_basis: Promoted from supporting to core because abstract explicitly describes this as 'training-free framework' that 'captures semantic shifts—pivotal moments of narrative change' for long video LVLMs — extremely close to STITCH's third application axis.

filename: TimeLoc_ A Unified End-to-End Framework for Precise Timestamp  Localization in Long Videos.pdf
title: TimeLoc: A Unified End-to-End Framework for Precise Timestamp Localization in Long Videos
authors: Chen-Lin Zhang, Lin Sui, Shuming Liu, Fangzhou Mu, Zhangcheng Wang, Bernard Ghanem
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PROMOTED to core in v2. Like STITCH, unifies temporal action localization, temporal video grounding, moment retrieval, AND GEBD in a single framework — but supervised. Strongest published reference for STITCH's multi-task framing claim.
evidence_basis: general_multi_task_framing=true: abstract explicitly lists temporal action localization, temporal video grounding, moment retrieval, and GEBD as covered subtasks — exactly the union STITCH targets, making it the closest multi-task analog.

title: Query-Dependent Video Representation for Moment Retrieval and Highlight Detection (QD-DETR)
authors: WonJun Moon, Sangeek Hyun, SangUk Park, Dongchan Park, Jae-Pil Heo
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Strong supervised baseline on QVHighlights (Table 2). DETR-style architecture conditioned on query.

title: MovieChat: From Dense Token to Sparse Memory for Long Video Understanding
authors: Enxin Song, Wenhao Chai, Guanhong Wang, Yucheng Zhang, et al. (Gaoang Wang corresponding)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long-video understanding via sliding window + cosine-similarity token merging. Methodologically related (both use cosine similarity on temporal features).

filename: When One Moment Isn't Enough_ Multi-Moment Retrieval with Cross-Moment Interactions.pdf
title: When One Moment Isn't Enough: Multi-Moment Retrieval with Cross-Moment Interactions
authors: Zhuo Cao, Heming Du, Bingqing Zhang, Xin Yu, Xue Li, Sen Wang
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Introduces QV-M2 dataset and FlashMMR; multi-moment retrieval extension of QVHighlights.
notes: U Queensland. Builds on QVHighlights for multi-moment evaluation.

filename: TAG_ A Simple Yet Effective Temporal-Aware Approach for Zero-Shot Video Temporal Grounding.pdf
title: TAG: A Simple Yet Effective Temporal-Aware Approach for Zero-Shot Video Temporal Grounding
authors: Jin-Seop Lee, SungJoon Lee, Jaehan Ahn, YunSeok Choi, Jee-Hyong Lee
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PROMOTED to core in v2. Zero-shot training-free VTG using temporal pooling + temporal coherence clustering — the clustering step is conceptually close to STITCH's CPD-based segmentation. Explicitly addresses 'semantic fragmentation' that STITCH's chunk-based formulation also addresses.
notes: v1 mistakenly described this as audio-visual grounding — it is purely visual zero-shot VTG using temporal coherence clustering, which is highly relevant to STITCH's segmentation philosophy.
evidence_basis: Promoted from supporting to core because actual paper reads: 'incorporates temporal pooling, temporal coherence clustering, and similarity adjustment' — temporal coherence clustering is essentially CPD-style segmentation, making this the methodologically closest training-free moment-retrieval method to STITCH.

filename: UniVTG_ Towards Unified Video-Language Temporal Grounding.pdf
title: UniVTG: Towards Unified Video-Language Temporal Grounding
authors: Kevin Qinghong Lin, Pengchuan Zhang, Joya Chen, Shraman Pramanick, Difei Gao, Alex Jinpeng Wang, Rui Yan, Mike Zheng Shou
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Unified VLM grounding pre-trained on multiple VTG tasks. Closest supervised analog to STITCH's multi-task framing on the moment retrieval side.
notes: NUS Show Lab + Meta AI + JHU. Multi-task grounding pretraining.

filename: UnLoc_ A Unified Framework for Video Localization Tasks.pdf
title: UnLoc: A Unified Framework for Video Localization Tasks
authors: Shen Yan, Xuehan Xiong, Arsha Nagrani, Anurag Arnab, Zhonghao Wang, Weina Ge, David Ross, Cordelia Schmid
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Unified localization framework (moment retrieval + highlight + action localization). Multi-task framing aligns with STITCH but supervised.

filename: SnAG_ Scalable and Accurate Video Grounding.pdf
title: SnAG: Scalable and Accurate Video Grounding
authors: Fangzhou Mu, Sicheng Mo, Yin Li
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Scalable late-fusion supervised grounding. Late fusion enables reusable representations across queries, making the compute style closer to STITCH than other query-conditioned methods.
notes: UW-Madison. Late fusion for scalable many-query grounding.
evidence_basis: reusable_compute_style classified as reusable_repr_plus_scoring (not rerun_query_specific) because abstract explicitly favors late fusion as 'more cost-effective' for many queries — implying reusable video-side encoding.

filename: BAM-DETR_ Boundary-Aligned Moment Detection Transformer for Temporal Sentence Grounding in Videos.pdf
title: BAM-DETR: Boundary-Aligned Moment Detection Transformer for Temporal Sentence Grounding
authors: Pilhyeon Lee, Hyeran Byun
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Boundary-aligned DETR for moment detection. Table 2 supervised competitor.

filename: SimBase_ A Simple Baseline for Temporal Video Grounding.pdf
title: SimBase: A Simple Baseline for Temporal Video Grounding
authors: Peijun Bao, Alex C. Kot
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Simple supervised baseline. Useful calibration that complex architectures aren't always needed.
notes: Same first author as Vid-Morp (Peijun Bao, NTU).

filename: Vid-Morp_ Video Moment Retrieval Pretraining from Unlabeled Videos in the Wild.pdf
title: Vid-Morp: Video Moment Retrieval Pretraining from Unlabeled Videos in the Wild
authors: Peijun Bao, Chenqi Kong, Zihao Shao, Boon Poh Ng, Meng Hwa Er, Alex C. Kot
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Unsupervised VMR pretraining from unlabeled videos with the ReCorrect algorithm. Adjacent to STITCH's 'no annotations' philosophy.
evidence_basis: unsupervised=true because pretraining uses pseudo-annotations from unlabeled wild videos with no human-curated moment labels.

filename: Point to Span_ Zero-Shot Moment Retrieval for Navigating Unseen Hour-Long Videos.pdf
title: Point to Span: Zero-Shot Moment Retrieval for Navigating Unseen Hour-Long Videos
authors: Mingyu Jeon, Jisoo Yang, Sungjin Han, Jinkwon Hwang, Sunjae Yoon, Jonghee Kim, Junyeoung Kim
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PROMOTED to core in v2. Zero-shot Long Video Moment Retrieval (ZLVMR), search-then-refine paradigm in hour-long videos. Closest published method to STITCH's training-free moment retrieval at long-video scale.
evidence_basis: long_video_framing=true: paper explicitly targets 'unseen hour-long videos' and ZLVMR. Promoted to core because it is the most direct competitor to STITCH's combination of training-free + long-video + moment retrieval.

filename: VideoTree_ Adaptive Tree-based Video Representation for LLM Reasoning on  Long Videos.pdf
title: VideoTree: Adaptive Tree-based Video Representation for LLM Reasoning on Long Videos
authors: Ziyang Wang, Shoubin Yu, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Jaehong Yoon, Feng Cheng, Gedas Bertasius, Mohit Bansal
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PROMOTED to core in v2. Training-free, query-adaptive hierarchical video representation for LLM reasoning. Closest published training-free competitor for STITCH App 3 (VLM long-video reasoning).
evidence_basis: training_free=true confirmed in abstract ('VideoTree, a training-free framework'). reusable_compute_style=rerun_query_specific because the tree itself is built query-adaptively per query.

filename: Moment Sampling in Video LLMs for Long-Form Video QA.pdf
title: Moment Sampling in Video LLMs for Long-Form Video QA
authors: Mustafa Chasmai, Gauri Jagatap, Gouthaman KV, Grant Van Horn, Subhransu Maji, Andrea Fanelli
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PROMOTED to core in v2. Training-free, query-agnostic frame/moment sampling for Video LLMs on long-form QA. Direct competitor for STITCH App 3.
evidence_basis: Promoted to core: directly matches STITCH App 3 axis (training-free, long video, VLM frame selection).

filename: AdaReTaKe_ Adaptive Redundancy Reduction to Perceive Longer for  Video-language Understanding.pdf
title: AdaReTaKe: Adaptive Redundancy Reduction to Perceive Longer for Video-language Understanding
authors: Xiao Wang, Qingyi Si, Jianlong Wu, Shiyu Zhu, Li Cao, Liqiang Nie
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Training-free adaptive token-level redundancy reduction for long-video VLMs. Adjacent to STITCH App 3 but operates at token-compression rather than chunk-segmentation level.

filename: LongVU_ Spatiotemporal Adaptive Compression for Long Video-Language Understanding.pdf
title: LongVU: Spatiotemporal Adaptive Compression for Long Video-Language Understanding
authors: Xiaoqian Shen, Yunyang Xiong, Changsheng Zhao, et al. (Meta AI + KAUST + Korea U)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised spatiotemporal compression for long video VLMs. Trained alternative to STITCH App 3.

filename: STORM_ Token-Efficient Long Video Understanding for Multimodal LLMs.pdf
title: STORM: Spatiotemporal TOken Reduction for Multimodal LLMs (Token-Efficient Long Video Understanding)
authors: Jindong Jiang, Xiuyu Li, Zhijian Liu, Muyang Li, Guo Chen, Zhiqi Li, et al. (NVIDIA-led)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Token-efficient long-video MLLM with Mamba SSM temporal encoder. Trained competitor for VLM frame/token efficiency.
notes: NVIDIA. v1 caveat about possible RL-paper confusion now resolved — confirmed video-MLLM paper.

filename: LVC_ A Lightweight Compression Framework for Enhancing VLMs in Long  Video Understanding.pdf
title: LVC: A Lightweight Compression Framework for Enhancing VLMs in Long Video Understanding
authors: Ziyi Wang, Haoran Wu, Yiming Rong, Deyang Jiang, Yixin Zhang, Yunlong Zhao, Shuang Xu, Bo Xu
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Lightweight VLM compression for long video. Adjacent to STITCH App 3 from the trained-compression side.

filename: Long Video Understanding with Learnable Retrieval in Video-Language Models.pdf
title: Long Video Understanding with Learnable Retrieval in Video-Language Models
authors: Jiaqi Xu, Cuiling Lan, Wenxuan Xie, Xuejin Chen, Yan Lu
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long video LVLM with learned retrieval over video tokens.

filename: AdaVideoRAG_ Omni-Contextual Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Efficient Long Video Understanding.pdf
title: AdaVideoRAG: Omni-Contextual Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Efficient Long Video Understanding
authors: unknown (not yet verified from PDF)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Adaptive RAG for long video understanding.
notes: Authors not verified — full title corrected in v2.

filename: Chapter-Llama_ Efficient Chaptering in Hour-Long Videos with LLMs.pdf
title: Chapter-Llama: Efficient Chaptering in Hour-Long Videos with LLMs
authors: Lucas Ventura, Antoine Yang, Cordelia Schmid, Gül Varol
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Hour-long video chaptering via LLMs in text domain. Trained alternative to STITCH's GEBD/segmentation approach for long-video structure.

filename: VideoINSTA_ Zero-shot Long Video Understanding via Informative Spatial-Temporal Reasoning with LLMs.pdf
title: VideoINSTA: Zero-shot Long Video Understanding via Informative Spatial-Temporal Reasoning with LLMs
authors: Ruotong Liao, Max Erler, Huiyu Wang, Guangyao Zhai, Gengyuan Zhang, Yunpu Ma, Volker Tresp
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot LLM-based long-video reasoning. Adjacent to STITCH App 3.

filename: Self-Chained Image-Language Model for Video Localization and Question Answering.pdf
title: Self-Chained Image-Language Model for Video Localization and Question Answering (SeViLA)
authors: Shoubin Yu, Jaemin Cho, Prateek Yadav, Mohit Bansal
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Multi-task localizer + QA from a frozen image-language backbone. Multi-task framing parallels STITCH (different in that it fine-tunes light heads).

filename: Measure Twice, Cut Once_ A Semantic-Oriented Approach to Video Temporal Localization with Video LLMs.pdf
title: Measure Twice, Cut Once: A Semantic-Oriented Approach to Video Temporal Localization with Video LLMs
authors: Zongshang Pang, Mayu Otani, Yuta Nakashima
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Semantic-oriented temporal localization with video LLMs; argues against direct timestamp generation. Different mechanism than STITCH but also semantically grounded.
notes: U Osaka + CyberAgent. Otani is also author on Uncovering Hidden Challenges.

filename: Prompts to Summaries_ Zero-Shot Language-Guided Video Summarization with Large Language and Video Models.pdf
title: Prompts to Summaries: Zero-Shot Language-Guided Video Summarization with Large Language and Video Models
authors: Mario Barbara, Alaa Maalouf
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot language-guided video summarization. Adjacent to STITCH's training-free philosophy.

filename: Aligning Moments in Time using Video Queries.pdf
title: Aligning Moments in Time using Video Queries (MATR)
authors: Yogesh Kumar, Uday Agarwal, Manish Gupta, Anand Mishra
closest_relation_to_our_paper: DEMOTED to peripheral in v2. Task is video-to-video moment retrieval (Vid2VidMR), not text-to-video moment retrieval — different task setup from STITCH's Table 2.
notes: v1 listed this as text-query moment retrieval; the actual paper uses VIDEO queries to localize moments in target videos.
evidence_basis: Demoted because the abstract explicitly defines the task as 'Video-to-video moment retrieval (Vid2VidMR)' with video queries, not natural-language queries — outside STITCH's Table 2 task formulation.

filename: TEMPURA_ Temporal Event Masked Prediction and Understanding for  Reasoning in Action.pdf
title: TEMPURA: Temporal Event Masked Prediction and Understanding for Reasoning in Action
authors: Jen-Hao Cheng, Vivian Wang, Huayu Wang, Huapeng Zhou, et al. (UW + CMU + NYCU + Microsoft)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Trained model that segments untrimmed video into events with dense descriptions and masked-event reasoning. Adjacent to GEBD framing but VLM-style reasoning rather than boundary localization.
notes: v1 mistitled this as 'Object-level Semantic Context for Temporal Sentence Grounding' — actual paper is masked event reasoning on the VER dataset (500K untrimmed videos). UW + CMU + NYCU + Microsoft.

filename: Modality-Aware Shot Relating and Comparing for Video Scene Detection.pdf
title: Modality-Aware Shot Relating and Comparing for Video Scene Detection (MASRC)
authors: Jiawei Tan, Hongxing Wang, Kang Dang, Jiaxin Li, Zhilong Ou
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised video scene detection (boundary detection between scenes via shot-relating). Adjacent to GEBD task; v1 mislabeled this as video QA.
notes: v1 incorrectly identified this as 'Modality-Aware Shot Relating and Reasoning for Video QA' — the actual paper is for Video Scene Detection.

filename: TriDet_ Temporal Action Detection with Relative Boundary Modeling.pdf
title: TriDet: Temporal Action Detection with Relative Boundary Modeling
authors: Dingfeng Shi, Yujie Zhong, Qiong Cao, Lin Ma, Jia Li, Dacheng Tao
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised temporal action detection with explicit boundary modeling. Background context for temporal localization with boundaries.

filename: Boundary-aware Self-supervised Learning for Video Scene Segmentation.pdf
title: Boundary-Aware Self-Supervised Learning for Video Scene Segmentation
authors: Jonghwan Mun, Minchul Shin, Gunsoo Han, Sangho Lee, Seongsu Ha, Joonseok Lee, Eun-Sol Kim
closest_relation_to_our_paper: SSL pretext tasks designed to learn boundary-aware features for video scene segmentation. Conceptually adjacent to GEBD (boundary detection without explicit annotations).
evidence_basis: unsupervised=true: paper explicitly uses self-supervised pretext tasks with no ground-truth annotations.

filename: Dense-Captioning Events in Videos.pdf
title: Dense-Captioning Events in Videos (ActivityNet Captions)
authors: Ranjay Krishna, Kenji Hata, Frederic Ren, Li Fei-Fei, Juan Carlos Niebles
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Source paper for ActivityNet Captions — STITCH Table 2 moment retrieval evaluation dataset.
notes: Stanford. Now upgraded to core because it's the underlying paper for a Table 2 dataset.

filename: Uncovering Hidden Challenges in Query-Based Video Moment Retrieval.pdf
title: Uncovering Hidden Challenges in Query-Based Video Moment Retrieval
authors: Mayu Otani, Yuta Nakashima, Esa Rahtu, Janne Heikkilä
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Analysis paper exposing trivial baselines and annotation biases on ActivityNet Captions / Charades-STA. Important for honest interpretation of STITCH Table 2 numbers.

filename: TALL_ Temporal Activity Localization via Language Query.pdf
title: TALL: Temporal Activity Localization via Language Query
authors: Jiyang Gao, Chen Sun, Zhenheng Yang, Ram Nevatia
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Foundational moment retrieval paper that defined the task formulation.

filename: Semantic Conditioned Dynamic Modulation for Temporal Sentence Grounding  in Videos.pdf
title: Semantic Conditioned Dynamic Modulation for Temporal Sentence Grounding in Videos (SCDM)
authors: Yitian Yuan, Lin Ma, Jingwen Wang, Wei Liu, Wenwu Zhu
closest_relation_to_our_paper: DEMOTED to peripheral in v2. Classic but pre-DETR-era TSG method; superseded by newer baselines that already cover STITCH's needs (QD-DETR, BAM-DETR, UniVTG).

filename: Local-Global Video-Text Interactions for Temporal Grounding.pdf
title: Local-Global Video-Text Interactions for Temporal Grounding (LGI)
authors: Jonghwan Mun, Minsu Cho, Bohyung Han
closest_relation_to_our_paper: DEMOTED to peripheral. Pre-DETR TSG baseline.

filename: Learning 2D Temporal Adjacent Networks for Moment Localization with Natural Language.pdf
title: Learning 2D Temporal Adjacent Networks for Moment Localization with Natural Language (2D-TAN)
authors: Songyang Zhang, Houwen Peng, Jianlong Fu, Jiebo Luo
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Classic 2D temporal map moment retrieval baseline. Important historical reference.

filename: Towards Balanced Alignment_ Modal-Enhanced Semantic Modeling for Video Moment Retrieval.pdf
title: Towards Balanced Alignment: Modal-Enhanced Semantic Modeling for Video Moment Retrieval
authors: Zhihang Liu, Jun Li, Hongtao Xie, Pandeng Li, Jiannan Ge, Sun-Ao Liu, Guoqing Jin
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Modal-enhanced semantic modeling for VMR. Standard supervised competitor.

filename: ChatVTG_ Video Temporal Grounding via Chat with Video Dialogue Large Language Models.pdf
title: ChatVTG: Video Temporal Grounding via Chat with Video Dialogue Large Language Models
authors: Mengxue Qu, Xiaodong Chen, Wu Liu, Alicia Li, Yao Zhao
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PROMOTED from peripheral to supporting. Zero-shot VTG via Video Dialogue LLMs generating multi-granularity segments — actually a relevant training-free baseline.
notes: BJTU + USTC. v1 had this as peripheral, but the abstract describes a training-free zero-shot pipeline that should be in supporting tier.

filename: MAD_ A Scalable Dataset for Language Grounding in Videos from Movie Audio Descriptions.pdf
title: MAD: A Scalable Dataset for Language Grounding in Videos from Movie Audio Descriptions
authors: Mattia Soldan, Alejandro Pardo, Juan León Alcázar, Fabian Caba Heilbron, Chen Zhao, Silvio Giancola, Bernard Ghanem
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Movie-scale language grounding dataset; not used in STITCH Table 2 but adjacent to long-video moment retrieval.

filename: MomentSeeker_ A Task-Oriented Benchmark For Long-Video Moment Retrieval.pdf
title: MomentSeeker: A Task-Oriented Benchmark for Long-Video Moment Retrieval
authors: Huaying Yuan, Jian Ni, Zheng Liu, Yueze Wang, Junjie Zhou, Zhengyang Liang, Bo Zhao, Zhao Cao, Zhicheng Dou, Ji-Rong Wen
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long-video moment retrieval benchmark. Not in STITCH Table 2 but adjacent.
notes: Renmin U + BAAI. Note Zheng Liu and Junjie Zhou also authors on MLVU — same lab cluster.

filename: LoVR_ A Benchmark for Long Video Retrieval in Multimodal Contexts.pdf
title: LoVR: A Benchmark for Long Video Retrieval in Multimodal Contexts
authors: Qifeng Cai, Hao Liang, Zhaoyang Han, Hejun Dong, Meiyi Qiang, Ruichuan An, Quanqing Xu, Bin Cui, Wentao Zhang, et al.
venue: year/venue unverified (ECNU + PKU + HUST + Beihang + OceanBase)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long video retrieval benchmark. Adjacent to STITCH's moment retrieval + long video setting but not directly in STITCH evaluation.

filename: EgoSchema_ A Diagnostic Benchmark for Very Long-form Video Language Understanding.pdf
title: EgoSchema: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Very Long-form Video Language Understanding
authors: Karttikeya Mangalam, Raiymbek Akshkulakov, Jitendra Malik
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long-form video QA benchmark derived from Ego4D. Context for STITCH Table 3.
notes: UC Berkeley. 250 hours of egocentric video with multi-choice QA.
